questions.question: Explain how force is related to motion and give examples.
questions.answer: Force is a push or pull that can change the state of motion of an object. It can start motion, stop motion, change speed or change the direction of motion.

questions.question: Explain in detail the different effects of force on objects.
questions.answer: Force can start motion, stop motion, change the speed of an object, change its direction and also change its shape when applied.

questions.question: Differentiate between contact and non-contact forces with detailed examples.
questions.answer: Contact forces act when objects touch each other such as friction and muscular force. Non-contact forces act without touching, such as gravitational and magnetic forces.

questions.question: Explain friction and discuss its advantages and disadvantages.
questions.answer: Friction is a force that opposes motion between surfaces in contact. It helps in walking and stopping vehicles but also causes wear and tear of machine parts.

questions.question: Explain how friction depends on the nature of surfaces in contact.
questions.answer: Friction is greater between rough surfaces because they have more irregularities that interlock, while smooth surfaces produce less friction.

questions.question: Explain gravitational force and describe its effects on objects on Earth.
questions.answer: Gravitational force is the force by which the Earth attracts objects toward its center. It causes objects to fall and gives them weight.

questions.question: Explain why objects thrown upward eventually fall back to the ground.
questions.answer: Objects thrown upward slow down due to Earth's gravitational pull and eventually fall back toward the Earth.

questions.question: Explain magnetic force and describe how magnets attract certain materials.
questions.answer: Magnetic force is the force exerted by magnets which attracts materials such as iron, nickel and cobalt due to magnetic fields.

questions.question: Explain how magnetic forces can act without direct contact between objects.
questions.answer: Magnetic forces act through magnetic fields that extend around magnets and influence magnetic materials even without touching them.

questions.question: Explain how friction helps in everyday activities.
questions.answer: Friction provides grip between surfaces allowing us to walk, write with a pencil and stop vehicles using brakes.

questions.question: Explain why lubrication reduces friction in machines.
questions.answer: Lubricants fill the irregularities between surfaces and create a smooth layer that reduces friction between moving parts.

questions.question: Explain why heavy vehicles require more force to move than lighter vehicles.
questions.answer: Heavy vehicles have greater mass and greater friction with the ground, so more force is required to start their motion.

questions.question: Explain how forces are used in sports activities.
questions.answer: Forces are applied when players hit, throw or kick balls, which changes the speed and direction of the objects involved.

questions.question: Explain how force can change the shape of objects with examples.
questions.answer: Applying force to objects such as clay or rubber bands changes their shape because the applied force deforms the material.

questions.question: Explain why friction can sometimes be harmful.
questions.answer: Friction causes wear and tear in machine parts and wastes energy as heat, reducing efficiency.

questions.question: Explain how gravitational force keeps objects on the surface of the Earth.
questions.answer: Earth's gravitational pull attracts objects toward its center, preventing them from floating away.

questions.question: Explain how forces help in the functioning of machines.
questions.answer: Machines use forces to move parts, lift loads and perform work efficiently.

questions.question: Explain why friction is greater when two rough surfaces slide over each other.
questions.answer: Rough surfaces have irregularities that interlock and resist motion, increasing friction.

questions.question: Explain how magnets are used in everyday life.
questions.answer: Magnets are used in compasses, electric motors, speakers and magnetic door catches.

questions.question: Explain how friction between tyres and roads helps vehicles move safely.
questions.answer: Friction between tyres and the road provides grip, allowing vehicles to move forward and stop safely.

questions.question: Explain the importance of gravitational force in the solar system.
questions.answer: Gravitational force keeps planets moving in orbits around the Sun and holds moons around planets.

questions.question: Explain how friction produces heat when two objects rub against each other.
questions.answer: When two surfaces rub, mechanical energy is converted into heat energy due to friction.

questions.question: Explain how forces influence the design of vehicles and machines.
questions.answer: Engineers design machines and vehicles by considering forces such as friction, gravity and air resistance to improve efficiency and safety.

questions.question: Explain how balanced and unbalanced forces affect motion.
questions.answer: Balanced forces do not change the motion of an object, while unbalanced forces cause changes in motion such as acceleration or direction change.

questions.question: Explain why understanding forces is important in science and engineering.
questions.answer: Understanding forces helps scientists and engineers design structures, machines and technologies that operate safely and efficiently.
